description: Chemistry professor Lily Tuckrige at Nitroba State University received harassment emails. The captured network traffic (~60MB pcap) revealed the perpetrator used Gmail webmail from an internal dormitory IP address, sending cookies in plaintext over HTTP.
tier_note: Ground truth verificable. Usar para scoring de precisión.

source.dataset: Digital Corpora - Nitroba University Harassment Scenario

artifacts.content: PCAP ~60MB | External source IP: 140.247.62.34 (student dormitory) | Internal NAT IP: 192.168.15.4 | MAC address: 00:17:f2:e2:c0:ce (Apple device) | User Agent: Firefox 2.0.0.16 / Mac OS X (dominant, 2463 requests); Safari 3.1.2 / Mac OS X (632); iTunes Mac (468); IE6/WinXP SP2 (475, minority) — machine is unambiguously Mac OS X 10.5.4 Leopard | Service used: willselfdestruct.com
forensic_anomalies: Gmail traffic with cookies sent in plaintext over HTTP
Use of willselfdestruct.com service to send anonymous harassment email

artifacts.content: Email #1: badguy@hotmail.com | Date: July 13 | Email #2: willselfdestruct.com | Message: 'you can't find us and you can't hide from us. Stop teaching. Start running.' | Identified account: jcoachj@gmail.com → Johnny Coach (Chem 109 student)
forensic_anomalies: Explicit threat message sent through self-destructing service
Gmail account jcoach@gmail.com was identified via plaintext cookies in the pcap

artifacts.content: Network setup: Three women share a dormitory (Alice, Barbara, Candice) | Password-less WiFi router installed by a friend | Ethernet provided by university | Student roster: Chemistry 109 available
forensic_anomalies: Password-less WiFi router allows anonymous and untraceable access
Combination of university Ethernet + unsecured WiFi complicates attribution
